description: A lightweight and flexible command-line JSON processor.

common_option.name: --args
tip: Consume remaining arguments as positional string values;

common_option.name: --ascii-output
tip: Output strings by only ASCII characters using escape sequences;

common_option.name: --binary
tip: Open input/output streams in binary mode;

common_option.name: --exit-status
tip: Set exit status code based on the output;

common_option.name: --from-file
tip: Load the filter from a file;

common_option.name: --indent
tip: Use n spaces for indentation (max 7 spaces);

common_option.name: --join-output
tip: Implies -r and output without newline after each output;

common_option.name: --jsonargs
tip: Consume remaining arguments as positional JSON values;

common_option.name: --null-input
tip: Use `null` as the single input value.

common_option.name: --raw-input
tip: Read each line as string instead of JSON;

common_option.name: --raw-output
tip: Output strings without escapes and quotes;

common_option.name: --raw-output0
tip: Implies -r and output NUL after each output;

common_option.name: --rawfile
tip: Set $name to string contents of file;

common_option.name: --slurp
tip: Read all inputs into an array and use it as the single input value;

common_option.name: --slurpfile
tip: Set $name to an array of JSON values read from the file;

common_option.name: --sort-keys
tip: Sort keys of each object on output;

common_option.name: --stream
tip: Parse the input value in streaming fashion;

common_option.name: --stream-errors
tip: Implies --stream and report parse error as an array;

common_option.name: --unbuffered
tip: Flush output stream after each output;
